meaning: n.弄虚作假,串改,伪造,歪曲
examples: This essay is a criticism on the logical basics of verification and falsification.
So what we have given there is a falsification of that not to the counter example to the argument.
Just recently we have collected evidence of falsification by Obama on his Selective Service Application.
Which all seems quite scientific: theories falsified, new ones offered up for falsification.
An obsession with the ways in which the history of Niagara Falls is a history of falsification, prevarication and omission.
